insert into `core_news_translations` (`locale`, `origin_id`, `title`, `content`, `create_user`, `updated_at`, `created_at`) values ('en', 188, 'Important regulations to know: Procedures for obtaining permission to camp overnight in a National Park.', '

To obtain a permit for overnight camping in a National Park, visitors must register directly at the Management Board or a ranger station, providing personal information, the number of people, and paying the prescribed lodging fee. Complying with the essential regulations on forest fire prevention and environmental protection is a mandatory condition for smoothly completing the procedures for obtaining an overnight camping permit in a National Park.

Why do you need to clearly understand the essential regulations and procedures for obtaining an overnight camping permit in a National Park?

Returning to nature, escaping the city's dust and smoke to enjoy the fresh air at nature reserves, is becoming a trend. However, a National Park is not an ordinary tourist destination; it is a sensitive ecosystem strictly protected by law.

Clearly understanding the essential regulations not only helps you avoid legal troubles or administrative penalties but also ensures your own safety. The procedures for obtaining an overnight camping permit in a National Park are designed so the Management Board can control the number of overnight visitors, enabling timely rescue plans in emergencies such as natural disasters, getting lost, or encountering wild animals. Moreover, complying with these rules is how we show respect for Mother Nature and contribute to preserving biodiversity for future generations.

What are the essential regulations on lodging activities in a National Park?

Before starting the journey, visitors should keep in mind the following core rules for a complete trip:

  • Permitted camping areas: Not every grassy patch in the forest is suitable for pitching a tent. You may only camp in administrative service zones or specific points designated by the Management Board.
  • Lodging hours: National Parks typically set start and end times for camping activities to ensure rangers can carry out their patrols.
  • Fire control: This is the most important regulation. Depending on the season (especially the dry season), lighting a campfire may be completely prohibited or only allowed in pre-prepared fire pits.
  • Waste management: The "Leave No Trace" principle is the guiding rule. All plastic waste and leftover food must be carried out of the forest.
  • Noise and light: To avoid disturbing the behavior of wildlife, you should limit loud noise after 10 p.m.

Mastering these essential regulations will help your procedures for obtaining an overnight camping permit in a National Park be approved more quickly when you work with the management staff.

What steps do the procedures for obtaining an overnight camping permit in a National Park involve?

Step 1: Contact and check destination information

About 3-7 days before your trip, contact the National Park's Management Board office (via hotline or official website). This is the first step in the procedures for obtaining an overnight camping permit to confirm whether the area is open to visitors, or whether it is in peak season / fire season.

Step 2: Prepare the lodging registration documents

Typically, the documents in the procedures for obtaining an overnight camping permit in a National Park are fairly simple, including:

  • List of participating members (full name, ID card/citizen ID/passport number).
  • Contact phone number of the group leader.
  • Planned itinerary (when you enter the forest, when you leave, and the travel route).
  • A commitment to comply with the essential regulations on forest protection.

Step 3: Register directly at the ranger station or entrance gate

Upon arrival, you need to go to the guidance office to report. There, the staff will verify your personal information and ask you to sign the lodging logbook. This is an indispensable part of the procedures for obtaining an overnight camping permit, aimed at ensuring forest security.

Step 4: Pay the fees and receive your lodging card

The fees usually include: a forest sightseeing fee (entrance ticket) and an overnight lodging fee. After completing payment, you will receive a receipt or a lodging card. Keep these documents carefully, as rangers may conduct spot checks at the campsite.

Step 5: Listen to the safety briefing from the rangers

Before you start moving into the camping area, the staff will go over the essential regulations specific to that area, such as sources of clean water, areas with phone signal, or animals to watch out for.

In addition, discover many more high-quality travel experiences at Vietnamtoure.com

Types of costs related to the procedures for obtaining an overnight camping permit in a National Park

The fees are not fixed and vary according to the regulations of each locality and the level of the National Park. However, you can refer to the following general price range:

  • Entrance ticket: Ranges from 20,000 - 60,000 VND/person.
  • Overnight lodging fee: Around 30,000 - 100,000 VND/person/night.
  • Environmental service fee: Some places charge an additional cleaning fee if you use the central campsites.
  • Guide/escort hire fee: For core zones or complex routes, this is a mandatory requirement to ensure safety and to complete the procedures for obtaining an overnight camping permit.

Note that properly following the procedures for obtaining an overnight camping permit in a National Park and paying the fees in full will give the conservation fund more resources to maintain and restore the landscape.

Safety and environmental protection tips you need to know

Once you have completed the essential regulations regarding paperwork, the field phase is when you need the most discipline.

  • Equip yourself with survival skills: Know how to pitch a sturdy tent, handle basic wounds, and use GPS positioning when there is no network.
  • Use dedicated trash bags: Prepare zip-lock bags to contain food odors that attract wild animals and to ensure no waste is dropped.
  • Respect wildlife: Absolutely do not feed the animals. Doing so erodes their natural hunting instincts and can be dangerous to you.
  • Appropriate clothing: Wear long, stretchy clothes and high-grip hiking shoes to move safely in the National Park.

 

Guidance on how to get your camping permit approved fastest

If you want to save time when carrying out the procedures for obtaining an overnight camping permit in a National Park, apply the following formula:

  • Email in advance: Draft a short plan and send it to the National Park Management Board's email.
  • Travel in small groups: Groups of 2-6 people are usually granted permits more easily than large parties (which require more procedures to assess environmental impact).
  • Go on weekdays: Avoid holidays when visitor numbers are overloaded; completing the procedures for obtaining an overnight camping permit will be much faster and easier.
  • Have your ID ready: Always carry the original for on-site verification.

 

Frequently asked questions about the regulations and procedures for obtaining an overnight camping permit

Is spontaneous camping allowed in a National Park?

Absolutely not. According to the essential regulations on forestry, spontaneous camping without going through the procedures for obtaining an overnight camping permit in a National Park is considered illegal trespassing and can be fined from a few million to tens of millions of dong.

Do I need to buy travel insurance before applying for a permit?

Although it is not required in the overnight camping permit application dossier, content experts always recommend having insurance for the best support in risky situations within the National Park.

Can children take part in overnight camping?

Yes, but parents must ensure their child's health and fully declare the information in the list when completing the procedures for obtaining an overnight camping permit in a National Park. Some remote areas may have age limits to ensure safety.

What should I do if I get lost in the forest?

As soon as you realize you are lost, stay put and use a whistle or signaling device. Since you have registered through the procedures for obtaining an overnight camping permit, the rangers will have information about the area you registered for in order to launch a search as quickly as possible.

Procedures for obtaining an overnight camping permit in a National Park

In short, enjoying the pristine beauty of the mountains and forests is a wonderful experience, but it comes with great responsibility. Complying with the essential regulations: procedures for obtaining an overnight camping permit in a National Park does not diminish the excitement of the trip; on the contrary, it gives you peace of mind and a guarantee of a civilized journey.

Be a responsible nature lover. Before you shoulder your backpack, take the time to thoroughly research the essential regulations of each locality to ensure that your footprints are the only thing you leave behind. We wish you a truly meaningful, soul-healing trip at Vietnam's beautiful National Parks!

LIEN MINH VIET International Trading Company Limited

Virenaretreat - Where Your Soul Is Healed !

Phone: 0986662282

Email: support@virenaretreat.com Website: virenaretreat.com

    <li dir="ltr" style="line-height: 1.38;"><span style="font-size: 11pt; font-family: Arial, sans-serif; color: #000000; background-color: transparent; font-weight: 400; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;">A commitment to comply with the </span><span style="font-size: 11pt; font-family: Arial, sans-serif; color: #000000; background-color: transparent; font-weight: bold; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;">essential regulations</span><span style="font-size: 11pt; font-family: Arial, sans-serif; color: #000000; background-color: transparent; font-weight: 400; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;"> on forest protection.</span></li>\r\n

After completing payment, you will receive a receipt or a lodging card. Keep these documents carefully, as rangers may conduct spot checks at the campsite.</span></p>\r\n
    <h3 dir="ltr" style="line-height: 1.38; margin-top: 14pt; margin-bottom: 4pt;"><span style="font-size: 13pt; font-family: Arial, sans-serif; color: #000000; background-color: transparent; font-weight: bold; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;">Step 5: </span><span style="font-size: 13pt; font-family: Arial, sans-serif; color: #000000; background-color: transparent; font-weight: 400; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;">Listen to the safety briefing from the rangers</span></h3>\r\n

    <ul>\r\n
    <li dir="ltr" style="line-height: 1.38;"><span style="font-size: 11pt; font-family: Arial, sans-serif; color: #000000; background-color: transparent; font-weight: 400; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;">Entrance ticket: Ranges from 20,000 - 60,000 VND/person.</span></li>\r\n

    </ul>\r\n

    <p dir="ltr" style="line-height: 1.38; margin-top: 12pt; margin-bottom: 12pt;"><span style="font-size: 11pt; font-family: Arial, sans-serif; color: #000000; background-color: transparent; font-weight: 400; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;">Once you have completed the </span><span style="font-size: 11pt; font-family: Arial, sans-serif; color: #000000; background-color: transparent; font-weight: bold; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;">essential regulations</span><span style="font-size: 11pt; font-family: Arial, sans-serif; color: #000000; background-color: transparent; font-weight: 400; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;"> regarding paperwork, the field phase is when you need the most discipline.</span></p>\r\n

    <li dir="ltr" style="line-height: 1.38;"><span style="font-size: 11pt; font-family: Arial, sans-serif; color: #000000; background-color: transparent; font-weight: 400; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;">Equip yourself with survival skills: Know how to pitch a sturdy tent, handle basic wounds, and use GPS positioning when there is no network.</span></li>\r\n
    <li dir="ltr" style="line-height: 1.38;"><span style="font-size: 11pt; font-family: Arial, sans-serif; color: #000000; background-color: transparent; font-weight: 400; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;">Use dedicated trash bags: Prepare zip-lock bags to contain food odors that attract wild animals and to ensure no waste is dropped.</span></li>\r\n
    <li dir="ltr" style="line-height: 1.38;"><span style="font-size: 11pt; font-family: Arial, sans-serif; color: #000000; background-color: transparent; font-weight: 400; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;">Respect wildlife: Absolutely do not feed the animals. Doing so erodes their natural hunting instincts and can be dangerous to you.</span></li>\r\n
    <li dir="ltr" style="line-height: 1.38;"><span style="font-size: 11pt; font-family: Arial, sans-serif; color: #000000; background-color: transparent; font-weight: 400; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;">Appropriate clothing: Wear long, stretchy clothes and high-grip hiking shoes to move safely in the </span><span style="font-size: 11pt; font-family: Arial, sans-serif; color: #000000; background-color: transparent; font-weight: bold; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;">National Park</span><span style="font-size: 11pt; font-family: Arial, sans-serif; color: #000000; background-color: transparent; font-weight: 400; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;">.</span></li>\r\n

    <p dir="ltr" style="line-height: 1.38; margin-top: 12pt; margin-bottom: 12pt;"><span style="font-size: 11pt; font-family: Arial, sans-serif; color: #000000; background-color: transparent; font-weight: 400; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;">If you want to save time when carrying out the </span><span style="font-size: 11pt; font-family: Arial, sans-serif; color: #000000; background-color: transparent; font-weight: bold; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;">procedures for obtaining an overnight camping permit in a National Park</span><span style="font-size: 11pt; font-family: Arial, sans-serif; color: #000000; background-color: transparent; font-weight: 400; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;">, apply the following formula:</span></p>\r\n
    <ul>\r\n
    <li dir="ltr" style="line-height: 1.38;"><span style="font-size: 11pt; font-family: Arial, sans-serif; color: #000000; background-color: transparent; font-weight: 400; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;">Email in advance: Draft a short plan and send it to the National Park Management Board's email.</span></li>\r\n
    <li dir="ltr" style="line-height: 1.38;"><span style="font-size: 11pt; font-family: Arial, sans-serif; color: #000000; background-color: transparent; font-weight: 400; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;">Travel in small groups: Groups of 2-6 people are usually granted permits more easily than large parties (which require more procedures to assess environmental impact).</span></li>\r\n
    <li dir="ltr" style="line-height: 1.38;"><span style="font-size: 11pt; font-family: Arial, sans-serif; color: #000000; background-color: transparent; font-weight: 400; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;">Go on weekdays: Avoid holidays when visitor numbers are overloaded; completing the </span><span style="font-size: 11pt; font-family: Arial, sans-serif; color: #000000; background-color: transparent; font-weight: bold; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;">procedures for obtaining an overnight camping permit</span><span style="font-size: 11pt; font-family: Arial, sans-serif; color: #000000; background-color: transparent; font-weight: 400; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;"> will be much faster and easier.</span></li>\r\n

    <p> </p>\r\n

    <h2 dir="ltr" style="line-height: 1.38; margin-top: 18pt; margin-bottom: 4pt;"><span style="font-size: 17pt; font-family: Arial, sans-serif; color: #000000; background-color: transparent; font-weight: bold; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;">Frequently asked questions about the regulations and procedures for obtaining an overnight camping permit</span></h2>\r\n

    <p dir="ltr" style="line-height: 1.38; margin-top: 12pt; margin-bottom: 12pt;"><span style="font-size: 11pt; font-family: Arial, sans-serif; color: #000000; background-color: transparent; font-weight: 400; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;">Absolutely not. According to the </span><span style="font-size: 11pt; font-family: Arial, sans-serif; color: #000000; background-color: transparent; font-weight: bold; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;">essential regulations</span><span style="font-size: 11pt; font-family: Arial, sans-serif; color: #000000; background-color: transparent; font-weight: 400; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;"> on forestry, spontaneous camping without going through the </span><span style="font-size: 11pt; font-family: Arial, sans-serif; color: #000000; background-color: transparent; font-weight: bold; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;">procedures for obtaining an overnight camping permit in a National Park</span><span style="font-size: 11pt; font-family: Arial, sans-serif; color: #000000; background-color: transparent; font-weight: 400; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;"> is considered illegal trespassing and can be fined from a few million to tens of millions of dong.</span></p>\r\n
    <h3 dir="ltr" style="line-height: 1.38; margin-top: 14pt; margin-bottom: 4pt;"><span style="font-size: 13pt; font-family: Arial, sans-serif; color: #000000; background-color: transparent; font-weight: bold; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;">Do I need to buy travel insurance before applying for a permit?</span></h3>\r\n
